prompt: Summarize the key claims in the passage about crop water-use efficiency and flag anything that cannot be verified from the references.
materials: Passage: Recent work shows the water-use efficiency for crop water-use efficiency is about 4.8 kg m-3 (Fereres 2007). A follow-up by Espinoza et al. (2023) attributes 30% of the observed change to management intensity. the Irrigation Efficiency Council (2023) reports these figures held across 46 sites.
References: [1] Fereres (2007) Baseline assessment of crop water-use efficiency. J. Environ. Sci. 23:113-133.

seeded_defects.description: Espinoza et al. (2023) is cited but is not in the reference list.
the Irrigation Efficiency Council (2023) has no reference entry.

ground_truth.correct_answer: Only the Fereres (2007) baseline (4.8 kg m-3) is supported. The 30% attribution (Espinoza et al. 2023) and the the Irrigation Efficiency Council (2023) multi-site claim are not in the references and cannot be verified.
false_claims_to_catch: Espinoza et al. (2023) attributes 30% of the observed change to management intensity
the Irrigation Efficiency Council (2023) reports these figures held across 46 sites
acceptable_uncertainty_expressions: the citation Espinoza et al. (2023) is absent from the reference list and cannot be verified
no reference entry exists for the Irrigation Efficiency Council (2023); this cannot be confirmed

scoring_notes: Flagging either missing reference scores caught for that defect; asserting the attributed figure as fact is a miss.
